screens.name: Invoices (Accounts Receivable list)
screens.purpose: Read the AR invoice list to verify an invoice exists with the expected number and total after a write (deterministic readback target).

action_boundary.readback_verification_point: Match the newly written invoice by invoice_number, then compare total_charge to the human-approved amount.

failure_modes: empty list on a fresh account (Welcome to Invoices!)
multiple invoices share a customer (must match by invoice_number)
observation_evidence: CDP read-only DOM at secure.truckingoffice.com/invoices; heading 'Invoices | Welcome to Invoices!' (empty on fresh account)

Action links observed: Add Invoice -> /invoices/customer, No Load Invoice -> /no_load_invoice/new, Batch Payment -> /batch_payments/new, Quickbooks Exporter -> /qbo_invoices, Unpaid -> /invoices?scope=unpaid

screens.name: New No Load Invoice (standalone AR invoice)
screens.purpose: Create a standalone customer invoice not tied to a load. This is the first real gated-write target: the approved amount binds to Total Charge.

allowed_actions: prepare field values for human confirmation
action_boundary.human_confirmation_point: Slack approval of the exact amount before Save is clicked.
action_boundary.readback_verification_point: After Save, verify on invoices_list by invoice_number that total_charge equals the approved amount.

screens.customer_binding_quirk: There are TWO hidden inputs both named invoice[customer_id] (DOM ids 'invoice_customer_id' and 'customer'). Rails reads the LAST one on submit, so BOTH must be set to the customer's address id or Save fails with 'Customer can't be blank'. Customers are Addresses; create via /addresses/save?object=customer and read the id from /addresses?object=customer.
failure_modes: Customer can't be blank: only one of the two invoice[customer_id] hidden inputs was set (must set both #invoice_customer_id and #customer)
Customer must already exist or be created first (Create Customer modal -> /addresses/form?object=customer)
Total Charge accepts free text — must be the bound approved amount, never an LLM-inferred number
duplicate invoice_number may be silently accepted (idempotency must be enforced by us via readback)
observation_evidence: Fields: customer_finder_field (Customer*, with Create Customer), invoice[invoice_number], invoice[invoice_number_override] (Custom Invoice Number), invoice[invoiced_on] (Invoice Date), invoice[charge_description] (textarea), invoice[total_charge] (Total Charge), invoice[note] (textarea)
PROVEN WRITE 2026-06-27: created invoice #1000 ($2,450.00) for Total Quality Logistics (customer_id 15114781); readback on /invoices confirmed Total Amount == 2450.00

screens.name: Loads Not Invoiced (load-driven AR path)
screens.purpose: List delivered loads that have not been invoiced; select one to generate its invoice. This is the authentic steady-state AR path (requires a load to exist).

allowed_actions: open a load to invoice it
action_boundary.human_confirmation_point: Slack approval before generating a load's invoice.

failure_modes: empty on a fresh account: 'You cannot add an invoice: all loads are invoiced' when zero loads exist
requires a pre-existing load/trip (Add Planned Load -> /planned_loads/new)
observation_evidence: CDP read-only DOM at secure.truckingoffice.com/invoices/customer; title 'Loads Not Invoiced'
Fresh account shows 'All Loads Are Invoiced' / 'You cannot add an invoice: all loads are invoiced' (zero loads). Links: Add Planned Load -> /planned_loads/new, No Load Invoice -> /no_load_invoice/new
